questions.q: What does the abbreviation UPSC stand for?
questions.explanation: UPSC stands for the Union Public Service Commission.

questions.q: What is the main function of the UPSC?
questions.explanation: The UPSC conducts examinations for recruitment to the central civil services and All India Services.

questions.q: The UPSC is best described as a:
questions.explanation: The UPSC is a constitutional body established under the Constitution of India.

questions.q: Under which Article of the Constitution is the UPSC established?
questions.explanation: The UPSC is established under Article 315 of the Constitution.

questions.q: Who appoints the Chairman and members of the UPSC?
questions.explanation: The Chairman and members of the UPSC are appointed by the President of India.

questions.q: The UPSC conducts the well-known examination to recruit officers to which premier service, among others?
questions.explanation: The UPSC conducts the Civil Services Examination, which recruits to services including the Indian Administrative Service.

questions.q: The UPSC handles recruitment at which level of government?
questions.explanation: The UPSC is the central recruiting agency at the Union level.

questions.q: The All India Services recruited through the UPSC serve both the Union and the:
questions.explanation: All India Services such as the IAS and IPS serve both the Union and the state governments.

questions.q: The UPSC primarily acts as a recruiting and advisory body in matters relating to:
questions.explanation: The UPSC recruits civil servants and advises the government on related personnel matters.

questions.q: The headquarters of the UPSC is located in which city?
questions.explanation: The UPSC has its headquarters in New Delhi.

questions.q: Which Part of the Constitution contains the provisions relating to Public Service Commissions?
questions.explanation: Provisions relating to the Union and State Public Service Commissions are in Part XIV of the Constitution.

questions.q: What is the term of office of a UPSC member?
options: Six years or until the age of 65, whichever is earlier
questions.explanation: A UPSC member holds office for six years or until reaching 65 years of age, whichever is earlier.

questions.q: Roughly what fraction of the members of the UPSC must be persons who have held office under the government for at least ten years?
questions.explanation: The Constitution requires that about half the members of the UPSC have at least ten years of government service.

questions.q: The expenses of the UPSC, including salaries and pensions, are charged on which fund?
questions.explanation: UPSC expenses are charged on the Consolidated Fund of India to secure its independence.

questions.q: To whom does the UPSC submit its annual report on its work?
questions.explanation: The UPSC presents its annual report to the President, who lays it before Parliament.

questions.q: A member of the UPSC can be removed from office by the President on the ground of misbehaviour only after an inquiry by the:
questions.explanation: For removal on grounds of misbehaviour, the matter must be referred to the Supreme Court for inquiry.

questions.q: The first major stage of the UPSC Civil Services Examination is commonly known as the:
questions.explanation: The Civil Services Examination begins with a Preliminary (objective) stage, followed by Mains and an interview.

questions.q: Which All India Service responsible for policing is filled through the UPSC examination?
questions.explanation: The Indian Police Service (IPS) is among the services filled through the UPSC examination.

questions.q: Besides the IAS and IPS, which All India Service deals with forests and is filled via the UPSC?
questions.explanation: The Indian Forest Service is the third All India Service, recruited through a UPSC examination.

questions.q: The UPSC also advises the government on disciplinary matters affecting persons serving under the Union in a:
questions.explanation: The UPSC advises the government on disciplinary matters relating to civil servants.

questions.q: Each state may have its own Public Service Commission; the UPSC operates at the level of the:
questions.explanation: The UPSC serves the Union, while State Public Service Commissions recruit for individual states.

questions.q: A Chairman or member of the UPSC, on ceasing to hold office, is generally restricted from further government employment to protect the body's:
questions.explanation: Post-tenure restrictions on re-employment safeguard the impartiality and independence of the UPSC.

questions.q: The UPSC traces its origin to a commission first set up in 1926 under the title:
questions.explanation: A Public Service Commission was first established in 1926, the institutional predecessor of today's UPSC.

questions.q: Which Article empowers the President to make regulations on the number of UPSC members and their conditions of service?
questions.explanation: Article 318 empowers the President to determine the number of UPSC members and their conditions of service.

questions.q: The functions of the UPSC are chiefly laid out in which Article of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 320 sets out the functions of the Public Service Commissions, including the UPSC.

questions.q: Under the Constitution, the UPSC came into being on its present footing with the commencement of the Constitution in which year?
questions.explanation: On the commencement of the Constitution on 26 January 1950, the body became the Union Public Service Commission.

questions.q: A vacancy in the office of the UPSC Chairman is filled by a member acting as Chairman, appointed by the:
questions.explanation: The President may appoint a member to act as Chairman of the UPSC during a vacancy in that office.

questions.q: The Constitution allows the scope of UPSC functions to be extended to additional matters by an Act of:
questions.explanation: Parliament may, by law, extend the functions of the UPSC under the constitutional scheme.

questions.q: Although the UPSC recruits and advises, the final decision to accept or reject its advice rests with the:
questions.explanation: The UPSC's role is advisory in many matters; the final decision on accepting its advice rests with the government.

questions.q: Two or more states may, with parliamentary approval, share a single commission known as a:
questions.explanation: The Constitution permits the creation of a Joint State Public Service Commission for two or more states by Parliament.
